subtasks.description: Evaluate whether the developer's access request for production database live debugging complies with security and access control policies. Check authorization level, approval requirements, audit trail needs, and any compliance constraints.

plan.cost_rationale: Single subtask required: This is an explicit policy evaluation scenario. The developer's request for production database access for debugging involves governance, access control, and compliance assessment—the core domain of policy_action. The task is assigned mid tier because it requires reasoning across multiple policy dimensions (authorization, approval workflows, compliance rules, audit requirements) rather than a simple single-policy check. No cross-source reconciliation, SQL query, or multi-tool orchestration is needed at this stage. The policy_action agent will determine approval/denial and any required conditions or escalations."

output.reason: dev-alice does not hold the on-call role and has not obtained Manager approval, violating POL-401. Additionally, POL-402 explicitly restricts debug access to designated staging environments, prohibiting live debugging on prod-mysql-primary. Both applicable policies are violated; access must be denied.
applied_constraints: Manager approval required before production database access can be granted
On-call role assignment required for production database access
Debugging activities must be redirected to the designated staging environment (not prod-mysql-primary)
Any future approved access must include full audit trail logging of all queries executed
Access request should be formally submitted through the change management process for review
